title: Drone Strike Kills 23 on Civilian Convoy in Ed Daein, East Darfur
detail: A drone strike hit a convoy of commercial vehicles in Ed Daein, East Darfur, killing at least 23 people including women and children. The attack came just hours after international condemnation of the March 20 Eid al-Fitr hospital strike in the same city. Both strikes in Ed Daein within 48 hours compounded the humanitarian catastrophe in East Darfur and intensified calls for accountability. UN OCHA documented the strike as part of a broader pattern of drone attacks on civilians and civilian infrastructure across Sudan.

title: Daily Drone Strikes on Kordofan Markets and Health Facilities
detail: Fighting intensified in Kordofan in early 2026 with almost daily drone strikes on markets, health facilities, and residential areas. The targeting of civilian infrastructure has accelerated the humanitarian catastrophe in the region, which is already facing critical famine risk alongside El Fasher and multiple areas in Darfur.

title: OHCHR: RSF Violations in El Fasher Capture Amount to War Crimes
detail: The UN Human Rights Office (OHCHR) published findings in February 2026 concluding that RSF violations during the capture of El Fasher amount to war crimes under international humanitarian law. Documented violations include murder, intentional attacks on civilians, starvation as a warfare method, sexual violence, and torture. These findings follow the UN Fact-Finding Mission conclusion that the RSF committed genocide against non-Arab Indigenous ethnic groups during the 18-month siege.

title: Two Famines Declared; 400,000 Facing Starvation
detail: Two famines have been formally declared in Sudan: El-Fasher (North Darfur) and Kadugli (South Kordofan). An additional 20+ areas in Darfur and Kordofan are at critical famine risk. Approximately 400,000 people face starvation across Sudan, making it the world's most severe hunger crisis. The UN describes Sudan as hosting the world's largest humanitarian and displacement crisis with 24.6 million people food insecure.
